title: Natural Remedies for Indigestion: 10 Best Herbal Teas
intro_desc: Herbal teas for indigestion are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of indigestion by soothing the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal teas for indigestion include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Matricaria chamomilla, Mentha x piperita, Peumus boldus, Lavandula angustifolia, Artemisia absinthium, and Silybum marianum. If left untreated, indigestion can lead to discomfort, bloating, and poor digestion, negatively impacting daily life, causing fatigue, reduced productivity, and strained relationships due to irritability and mood swings.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a popular remedy for indigestion. This is because ginger has natural properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system. One of the main reasons ginger tea is good for indigestion is that it reduces nausea and vomiting. Ginger has compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which help to ease stomach discomfort and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. When you drink ginger tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can help to calm your stomach and reduce the symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, ginger tea can also help to stimulate digestion and move food through the digestive system more quickly, which can help to prevent indigestion from occurring in the first place.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for indigestion because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the stomach and improve digestion.

Shogaol is good for indigestion because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to move through the system more easily and relieving symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.

Polyphenols, also known as cumin, are good for indigestion because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing symptoms of indigestion and discomfort.

Zingiberene is good for indigestion because it helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, relaxes the muscles, and eases the symptoms of nausea and bloating, making it easier to digest food.

Tannins, commonly known as carom seeds, are good for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by indigestion.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for indigestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea that helps with indigestion, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: 1 teaspoon of Cuminum cyminum seeds and 1 cup of boiling water. Begin by rinsing the Cuminum cyminum seeds in cold water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, add the seeds to a cup and pour in the boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has cooled slightly and the seeds have lost some of their flavor. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into another cup, discarding the seeds. You can add a sweetener like honey if desired, but be sure to use a small amount as the tea is already quite potent. Once the tea has cooled, you can drink it as needed to help soothe indigestion and ease any discomfort in the stomach. It is recommended to consume the tea in moderation and to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea is a natural remedy for indigestion because it has soothing properties that calm the stomach. The active compound licorice root in the tea works by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. This helps to ease nausea, heartburn, and bloating that often come with indigestion. The anti-inflammatory effect of licorice root also helps to heal the stomach lining, which can become irritated and inflamed due to acid reflux or other digestive issues. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea has been shown to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, which helps to protect the stomach lining and prevent acid from getting through. This natural process can help to alleviate discomfort and provide relief from indigestion symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for indigestion because it helps to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, allowing the digestive system to recover and heal faster, and also helps to increase the production of stomach acid, which aids in breaking down food properly.
Flavonoids, commonly known as cumin, are good for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort in the stomach.

Saponins (Cumin) are good for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and discomfort.
Polyphenols, also known as cumin, help to relieve indigestion by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, and also by enhancing digestive enzymes to break down food more efficiently.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for indigestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea, start by gathering 1 teaspoon of dried cumin seeds, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, add the cumin seeds to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the seeds and discard the solids. You can now drink the tea hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired. To help with indigestion, drink 1 cup of the tea after meals, ideally 2-3 times a day. As cumin seeds have a strong flavor, you may want to start with a small amount and adjust to taste. Additionally, you can also add a pinch of salt to enhance the flavor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare tea, also known as fennel tea, is a natural remedy for indigestion. This is because fennel seeds have a compound called anethole, which helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines. When we eat, our stomach muscles can get tight and cause discomfort. Fennel tea helps to calm these muscles down, making it easier for food to move through the digestive system. Additionally, fennel tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. This can be especially helpful for people who experience acid reflux or heartburn after eating. Drinking fennel tea after a meal may also help to ease bloating and gas, as it can help to break down the proteins in food more efficiently.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for indigestion because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food.
Polyphenols are good for indigestion because they help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, allowing food to move through the digestive system more smoothly.

Tannins, also known as Cumin, are good for indigestion because they help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, and also have a soothing effect on the digestive system, which in turn helps to ease symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and nausea.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea for indigestion, start by gathering the seeds of the Cuminum cyminum plant, also known as cumin seeds. Rinse 1-2 teaspoons of the seeds in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine the seeds with 1 cup of boiling water in a small saucepan. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the seeds to release their active compounds. Strain the tea into a cup using the fine-mesh strainer and discard the seeds. You can add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Alternatively, you can also make a cold infusion by steeping the cumin seeds in a cup of cold water for several hours in the refrigerator. Both methods can be used to create a soothing and digestive-friendly tea to help alleviate indigestion symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a popular natural remedy for indigestion. The main reason it's good for indigestion is that it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation. When we eat, our stomach muscles contract to mix food with digestive juices, but sometimes these muscles can contract too much, causing discomfort and pain. Peppermint tea's active ingredients, called menthol and menthone, calm down these muscles, making it easier for food to digest. This can also help to reduce nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce irritation. Drinking peppermint tea after meals may also help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for indigestion because it helps to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can ease symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.

Menthol is good for indigestion because it helps to ease digestive issues by relaxing the stomach muscles and reducing inflammation, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.

Tannins, also known as cumin, are good for indigestion because they help to soothe and calm the stomach by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria that can cause discomfort and nausea.

Quercetin is good for indigestion because it helps reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can cause heartburn and other digestive problems, and also has natural anti-oxidant properties that protect against cell damage.
Luteolin is good for indigestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach, making it easier to digest food.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea that helps with indigestion, start by gathering a handful of caraway seeds, which are the seeds of the Cuminum cyminum plant. Rinse the seeds gently in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water, then pat them dry with a paper towel. Combine the caraway seeds with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the seeds to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has cooled down to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into another cup, discarding the seeds, and add a drizzle of honey if desired. The tea should be consumed while still warm, as the heat can help to ease digestion. It is recommended to drink the caraway tea after meals or when experiencing indigestion symptoms, as it is said to have natural properties that can help to soothe the stomach and alleviate discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tea is good for indigestion because it has properties that help to ease stomach problems. The tea contains a chemical called quinine, which helps to calm down the stomach and reduce inflammation. This can help to relieve symptoms of indigestion, such as a burning sensation or bloating. When you drink Cinchona officinalis tea, the quinine works to reduce the production of stomach acid, which can help to prevent heartburn and other discomforts. Additionally, the tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce irritation. This can make it easier to digest food and help to calm down an upset stomach. The tea may also help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can help to move food through the digestive system more smoothly.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea, you will need dried Cuminum cyminum seeds, which are commonly known as cumin seeds. Start by boiling one cup of water in a saucepan and then add a teaspoon of dried Cuminum cyminum seeds. Reduce the heat to a low simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Next, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth to separate the seeds from the liquid. You can then discard the seeds and add a sweetener like honey or sugar if desired. Cuminum cyminum seeds have natural carminative properties that help to ease indigestion and relieve bloating. Drinking a cup of this herbal tea after meals can help to calm your stomach and promote healthy digestion. Be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anethum graveolens, also known as dill weed, is a natural remedy for indigestion. The tea made from its leaves and stems can help soothe the stomach and ease digestive discomfort. The main active compound in dill weed is dill apiole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the digestive system. This can be especially helpful for people who experience bloating, gas, and stomach pain after eating. The tea is also known to aid in digestion by stimulating the production of digestive enzymes, which helps break down food properly. Additionally, dill weed has been traditionally used to reduce nausea and vomiting, making it a potential remedy for morning sickness and other stomach-related issues. Drinking dill weed tea may also help reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as abdominal cramps and diarrhea.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea, which helps with indigestion, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Cuminum cyminum seeds, which are also known as cumin seeds, and boiling water. Begin by measuring one teaspoon of dried cumin seeds for every cup of water you plan to use. Then, in a saucepan, bring the water to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and add the measured cumin seeds to the water. Let the cumin seeds steep in the water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid. After the steeping time has passed, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the cumin seeds. Your Cuminum cyminum herbal tea is now ready to drink, and you can enjoy it hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired. To get the full benefits, it's recommended to drink the tea after meals to help with digestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum tea, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, has been used for centuries to help with indigestion. The tea is made from the bark of the Ceylon cinnamon tree, which contains a chemical called cinnamaldehyde. This chemical helps to ease stomach upset by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that can cause indigestion. When you drink Cinnamomum verum tea, the cinnamaldehyde is absorbed into your bloodstream and reaches your stomach, where it helps to soothe the lining and reduce irritation. The tea also helps to relax the muscles in your digestive system, allowing food to move through your stomach and intestines more smoothly. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum tea has natural antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm cramps and spasms in the stomach and intestines that can contribute to indigestion. By reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and relaxing digestive muscles, Cinnamomum verum tea can provide quick relief from indigestion and help to prevent future episodes.

Begin by boiling a cup of water in a kettle or pot, then add a teaspoon of dried cumin seeds to the boiling water. Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the seeds to release their medicinal properties. Next, remove the pot from the heat and let it steep for another 2-3 minutes. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, and discard the cumin seeds. If desired, add a sweetener like honey or sugar to taste. Finally, drink the tea warm, ideally after meals or when experiencing indigestion symptoms. This herbal tea is believed to help alleviate indigestion and soothe the digestive system due to the antispasmodic and carminative properties of cumin, making it a natural and soothing remedy for digestive issues.

This can be especially helpful for people who have liver problems or have taken medications that can harm the liver. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea for indigestion, start by gathering dried carom seeds, also known as ajwain seeds or Cuminum cyminum. Begin by boiling 1 cup of water in a pot, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried carom seeds. Reduce the heat to a low simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the seeds to release their flavors and oils into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the seeds. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's not necessary. Once the tea has cooled slightly, drink it slowly to help alleviate indigestion and other digestive issues. It's recommended to drink the tea after meals or whenever you experience indigestion symptoms. Note that Cuminum cyminum herbal tea is not suitable for pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as people with certain health conditions, so consult with a healthcare professional before consuming it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla tea, also known as German chamomile tea, is a popular remedy for indigestion. The tea is made from the dried flowers of the chamomile plant, which have been used for centuries to soothe digestive problems. The main active ingredients in chamomile tea are apigenin and luteolin, which are anti-inflammatory compounds that can help calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. When you drink chamomile tea for indigestion, the apigenin and luteolin bind to receptors in the stomach, which helps to relax the muscles and reduce spasms. This can help to ease symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. Chamomile tea also has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate indigestion. Additionally, chamomile tea has been shown to have a protective effect on the digestive tract, helping to prevent stomach ulcers and other digestive problems.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cuminum cyminum, also known as cumin, is a type of spice that has been used for centuries to help with digestive issues. The tea made from cumin seeds can be very beneficial for indigestion. This is because cumin has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the stomach and intestines. When you have indigestion, the stomach and intestines can become inflamed, leading to pain and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in cumin tea can help to soothe and calm these areas, making it easier to digest food. Cumin tea also contains compounds that help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can become tense when you're experiencing indigestion. By relaxing these muscles, cumin tea can help to ease the cramping and discomfort associated with indigestion. Additionally, cumin tea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent bacterial overgrowth in the stomach and intestines, further contributing to indigestion. Drinking cumin tea can help to ease the symptoms of indigestion and promote a sense of calm and well-being. Cumin tea is also easy to make, and can be prepared by steeping cumin seeds in hot water, or by adding cumin powder to a warm tea.

Polyphenols, also known as cumin, are good for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce swelling, making it easier to digest food and relieve discomfort and pain.

remedies.image_alt: Cuminum cyminum herbal teas for indigestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cuminum cyminum herbal tea for indigestion relief, start by gathering 1 teaspoon of Cuminum cyminum seeds, also known as cumin seeds, for every 8 ounces of water you will be using. Rinse the seeds in a fine-mesh sieve under cold running water, then gently pat them dry with a paper towel to remove excess moisture. Next, combine the seeds with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has turned a light brown color and the aroma of the seeds has been released. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to catch the seeds, then discard the seeds. You can add honey or lemon to the tea to taste, if desired. Once the tea has cooled slightly, you can drink it as needed for relief from indigestion and other digestive issues.

supplementary_best: A combination of Foeniculum vulgare (fennel), Zingiber officinale (ginger), and Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root) herbal teas can help remedy indigestion. Foeniculum vulgare is a natural anti-inflammatory and relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, easing cramps and discomfort. Zingiber officinale is a well-known digestive aid that stimulates digestion, improves appetite, and reduces nausea. Glycyrrhiza glabra has anti-inflammatory properties and soothes the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. This combination works well together because Foeniculum vulgare and Zingiber officinale's digestive properties complement Glycyrrhiza glabra's soothing effects, creating a powerful and balanced remedy for indigestion.
supplementary_avoid: If you have indigestion, it's best to avoid certain herbal teas that can make it worse. One such tea is peppermint tea, which can relax the muscles in your stomach and worsen acid reflux. Another tea to avoid is licorice root tea, as it can increase the production of stomach acid, leading to more discomfort. You should also avoid hawthorn tea, as it can stimulate digestion and worsen indigestion symptoms. Additionally, teas made from the leaves of the yarrow plant (Achillea millefolium) can cause stomach upset in some people and make indigestion worse. Tea sage (Salvia officinalis) can also stimulate digestion and worsen symptoms. Finally, teas containing the leaves of the pennyroyal plant (Mentha pulegium) can be toxic in large amounts and worsen stomach issues when consumed in moderation.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger is a natural remedy that helps with indigestion. It works by easing stomach contractions and reducing inflammation. When you eat ginger, its active compounds travel to your stomach, where they help calm down spasms and ease discomfort. Ginger is often consumed as a juice, added to food, or taken as a supplement.  Another natural remedy is apple cider vinegar, which helps regulate digestion. Drinking apple cider vinegar after meals can help balance your body's pH levels and reduce stomach acid production. This can ease symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and discomfort. Apple cider vinegar also contains probiotics, which support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.  Probiotics are also found in fermented foods like yogurt and kefir. These foods contain live microorganisms that help populate your gut with beneficial bacteria. A healthy balance of gut bacteria is essential for proper digestion. When your gut is balanced, you're less likely to experience indigestion and other digestive issues.  Baking soda can also help alleviate indigestion symptoms. When you mix baking soda with water, it forms a natural antacid that helps neutralize stomach acid. This can provide quick relief from heartburn and indigestion. However, it's essential to use baking soda in moderation, as excessive consumption can lead to an imbalance of electrolytes in the body.  Aloe vera juice is another natural remedy that helps with indigestion. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera juice can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. This can ease symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and discomfort. Aloe vera juice is often consumed after meals to help regulate digestion and alleviate indigestion symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have indigestion often experience other ailments like bloating, gas, and stomach pain. This is because indigestion is often caused by an imbalance in gut bacteria, which can lead to inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. Medicinal herbs like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile can help heal these ailments. Peppermint, for example, has a calming effect on the digestive system and can help relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and cramps. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the digestive tract. Chamomile, on the other hand, has a soothing effect on the digestive system and can help calm digestive spasms and reduce inflammation. These herbs can also help improve digestion and reduce symptoms like bloating and gas by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.  Additionally, people who experience indigestion may also experience heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach ulcers. These conditions can be caused by an overproduction of stomach acid, which can irritate the esophagus and digestive tract. Medicinal herbs like licorice root and slippery elm can help heal these ailments. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, while slippery elm can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. These herbs can also help reduce symptoms like heartburn and acid reflux by reducing the production of stomach acid and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.  People who experience indigestion may also experience constipation, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). These conditions can be caused by an imbalance in gut bacteria, which can lead to changes in bowel movements and digestive function. Medicinal herbs like aloe vera and triphala can help heal these ailments. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, while triphala can help promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and improve digestion. These herbs can also help regulate bowel movements and reduce symptoms like constipation and diarrhea.  It's worth noting that some people may also experience other ailments like fatigue, brain fog, and skin problems, which can be linked to indigestion. This is because the gut and the brain are connected through the gut-brain axis, and an imbalance in gut bacteria can lead to changes in mood, cognitive function, and skin health. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and turmeric can help heal these ailments by reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
